1.description: Investment capital and lending This graph plots the sum of all membership and paid-in capital at Corporate Credit Unions owned by CUs less assessments levied by the NCUA (left y-axis). The right y-axis is total lending by all CUs.

2.description: Heterogeneity in responses by investment capital share of nonloan assets These plots display point estimates obtained by estimating the baseline specification of Equation (1) within subsets of the data. The bars represent 95% confidence intervals. The subsets are constructed by splitting the sample into terciles based on the CU’s investment capital share of nonloan assets. Estimation uses the same set of controls, fixed effects, and clustering as the last column of Table 1.

3.description: Impulse response functions for deposit growth and deposit spreads The outcome variables are the change in logged deposits (left) and the deposit spread (right). These graphs plot quarterly impulse response functions to changes in the 2-year Treasury rate and asset losses (plus their interaction). Estimates are obtained by local projections. Estimation uses the same set of controls, fixed effects, and clustering as the last column of Table 1. Specifically, I estimate same second-stage Equation (1) but replace the outcome variable with various leads. The first- stage equation is the same.
